title: Born at Lumbini
description: Tradition places the birth of Siddhartha Gautama at Lumbini, near Kapilavastu, in the 6th or 5th century BCE.

title: Raised as a Shakya Prince
description: Buddhist tradition describes Siddhartha as the son of Shuddhodana of the Shakya clan.

title: Prophecy of Two Paths
description: Traditional biographies say sages predicted he would become either a universal ruler or a great spiritual teacher.

title: Sheltered Youth
description: Later Buddhist biographies portray Siddhartha's youth as protected from suffering and death.

title: Marries Yasodhara
description: Tradition identifies Yasodhara as Siddhartha's wife before his renunciation.

title: Rahula Is Born
description: Traditional sources say Siddhartha's son Rahula is born shortly before or around the time of the Great Renunciation.

title: Encounters the Four Sights
description: Buddhist tradition says he sees an old man, a sick person, a corpse, and an ascetic, awakening his religious quest.

title: Great Renunciation
description: Siddhartha leaves his home life to seek liberation from suffering.

title: Studies with Alara Kalama
description: Early Buddhist sources say Siddhartha studies meditative practices under the teacher Alara Kalama.

title: Studies with Uddaka Ramaputta
description: He continues his search under another teacher, Uddaka Ramaputta, but remains unsatisfied.

title: Practices Extreme Asceticism
description: Tradition says Siddhartha spends years in severe austerities with a group of ascetics.

title: Rejects Extreme Asceticism
description: He abandons severe self-mortification and turns toward the middle way.

title: Receives Sujata's Offering
description: Traditional accounts say Sujata offers him food before his final meditation at Bodh Gaya.

title: Enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree
description: At Bodh Gaya, Siddhartha attains awakening and becomes the Buddha.

title: Defeats Mara
description: Buddhist tradition recounts the Buddha's victory over Mara's temptations on the night of enlightenment.

title: Seven Weeks near the Bodhi Tree
description: Tradition says the newly awakened Buddha remains near Bodh Gaya for several weeks after enlightenment.

title: First Sermon at Sarnath
description: At Deer Park in Sarnath, the Buddha teaches the Four Noble Truths and the Noble Eightfold Path.

title: First Five Disciples
description: The five ascetics who had known Siddhartha become the Buddha's first disciples.

title: Sangha Founded
description: The monastic community begins to form around the Buddha and his earliest followers.

title: Yasa and Other Early Converts
description: Early narratives describe the conversion of Yasa and other early lay and monastic followers.

title: Sariputta and Moggallana Join
description: Two of the Buddha's most important disciples, Sariputta and Moggallana, join the sangha.

title: Supported by King Bimbisara
description: Tradition recounts royal support from Bimbisara of Magadha, helping stabilize the early community.

title: Returns to Kapilavastu
description: Traditional biographies say the Buddha returns to his home region and teaches members of his family.

title: Rahula Ordained
description: The Buddha's son Rahula is ordained and becomes one of the best-known figures in the early sangha.

title: Order of Nuns Established
description: Tradition says Mahapajapati Gotami becomes the first Buddhist nun after the Buddha permits an order of nuns.

title: Long Residence at Jetavana
description: The Buddha spends many rainy seasons teaching at Jetavana monastery near Shravasti.

title: Devadatta's Challenge
description: Tradition tells of a major dispute when Devadatta challenges the Buddha and attempts to create a schism.

title: Begins Final Journey
description: In the final phase of his life, the Buddha travels through Vaishali and other places before reaching Kushinagar.

title: The Last Meal
description: Traditional sources recount a final meal at Pava shortly before the Buddha's death.

title: Parinirvana at Kushinagar
description: The Buddha dies at Kushinagar and enters final nirvana; traditional chronologies often place this in the 5th century BCE.

title: Cremation and Distribution of Relics
description: After the Buddha's death, his body is cremated and his relics are distributed among different communities.
